name: Stegosaurus
description: A herbivorous dinosaur known for the plates on its back and spikes on its tail. Its name means 'roof lizard'.

name: Diplodocus
description: One of the longest dinosaurs with a very long tail that could be used like a whip.

name: Allosaurus
description: A large bipedal predator with powerful jaws and small crests above its eyes.

name: Brachiosaurus
description: One of the tallest dinosaurs. Its front legs were longer than the back legs, allowing it to reach tall trees.

name: Dilophosaurus
description: A predator known for its two semicircular crests on the skull. It was possibly capable of spitting venom.

name: Compsognathus
description: One of the smallest dinosaurs, about the size of a chicken. It fed on insects and small lizards.

name: Brontosaurus
description: A huge long-necked dinosaur that fed on plants. Often depicted with its neck held high.

name: Archaeopteryx
description: A transitional form between dinosaurs and birds, it had feathers but also teeth and a long tail.

name: Megalosaurus
description: One of the first described dinosaurs, a large predator that shaped many early ideas of dinosaurs.

name: Apatosaurus
description: A huge herbivorous dinosaur with a long neck and tail. Its name means 'deceptive lizard'.

name: Paluxysaurus
description: A large sauropod with a long neck, a herbivore that lived in what is now North America.

name: Triceratops
description: A herbivorous dinosaur known for its three facial horns and a bony frill protecting its neck.
